summary: Shard cache eviction storm during peak restore load
description: Three concurrent restores on the same host caused the shard cache to thrash, evicting and re-fetching the same shards repeatedly. Restore times tripled during the window.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: The cache byte budget in src/checkpoint/shardCache.ts is per-process, not per-host, so three restores on one host effectively divided the budget three ways without knowing it. That's the thrash.

comments.author: Sam Whitfield
comments.body: same thing happened to me once, coffee helped

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Mitigated by capping concurrent restores per host to one in the scheduler config. Longer term the cache budget should be host-aware, filed separately. Closing this as resolved.

comments.author: Maya Okafor
comments.body: Confirmed restore times back to baseline after the cap. Thanks Priya.
